title: Using Ai for strategie building
author: MR_M1997
selftext:
For the past year I've been using chatgpt for creating indicator and strategy scripts in Pine script. So far i'm getting quite some positive results on how it is going and it's overall fun to think and keep trying new ideas. 

I was wondering if chatgpt is the right A.I for it though. Is there anyone who could tell if other models are better for this type of scripting and coming up with the logic/ good understanding with given logic.

comments.author: QuantVault_AI
comments.body:
Pine Script + ChatGPT is a solid starting point but you'll 
hit a ceiling fast. Here's why and what to consider:

ChatGPT is great for:
- Writing Pine Script syntax (it knows the language well)
- Brainstorming indicator combinations
- Debugging code quickly

Where it falls short:
- It can't backtest for you. It'll write a strategy that 
  LOOKS good but has no idea if it actually works on real data.
- It hallucinates Pine Script functions that don't exist.
- It optimizes for what sounds smart, not what makes money.

What I'd suggest instead of staying in Pine Script:

Move to Python. Seriously. The jump is worth it because:
1. You can train actual ML models on your data (not just 
   rule-based indicators)
2. You can backtest properly with real spread/slippage
3. You can run it live against exchange APIs (Binance, Alpaca)
4. Feature engineering — 200+ features vs 5-6 indicators

I started with simple indicators too. Now I run 6 ML models 
analyzing 226 features per symbol across 9 timeframes. 
640+ trades, 62% win rate. Couldn't have done that in 
Pine Script.

For the AI tool question specifically:
- ChatGPT/Claude — good for writing code and logic
- But don't ask AI to DESIGN your strategy. Use it to 
  IMPLEMENT your ideas. The strategy should come from 
  data analysis, not a chatbot's suggestion.

The best workflow: analyze data in Python → find patterns → 
build features → train models → deploy. Use AI as your 
coding assistant, not your strategy designer.

replies.author: WalrusExtension3562
replies.body: Been using Claude code + autoresearch + ml stack. It creates python scripts for strategy, training, back testing etc. Connected it with kite and groww mcp servers for data. I give it the baseline strategy and it enhances it.

comments.author: Intelligent-Mess71
comments.body:
Big factor is the model matters less than how clear your rules are. Any AI can generate a strategy, but loose logic usually just looks good in backtests.

For instance, if you don’t factor in slippage or execution, the results won’t hold up live.

Most people end up overfitting without realizing it, that’s where it breaks.

Are you running them live or still testing?

replies.author: QuantVault_AI
replies.body:
100% agree on the overfitting point. Backtests lie beautifully.

Running both  paper trading for 6 weeks (640+ trades) and just started live with small capital this week. The paper results gave me enough confidence in the risk management to put real money in.

The slippage gap is real but smaller than I expected on crypto futures. Binance taker fee is 0.04%, and with limit orders on entries you can often get maker rate. 

The bigger gap is psychological watching real money 
move differently than paper numbers.

Overfitting is exactly why I use 6 different model types. If XGBoost and Ridge both agree on a trade, it's less likely to be a pattern that only exists in training data. 

Single model = overfit risk. Ensemble = consensus.

comments.author: FormalAd7367
comments.body: i feel like if we want to succeed, we have to group all of our collective intelligences to build one exclusive program for the group

replies.author: QuantVault_AI
replies.body:
Interesting idea but hard in practice. The challenge is 
everyone has different risk tolerance, capital, and 
time horizon. 

What works better is one system, transparent results. 
People can follow the signals and size according to 
their own risk. That's basically what hedge funds do 
one strategy, multiple investors.

comments.author: gabrigoo
comments.body: I have been using this thing called investi. I text it on imessage and basically finds good setups and paper trades them. if i think something could actually be good i copy trade it on my robinhood account [https://tryinvesti.co/u/gabrigoo](https://tryinvesti.co/u/gabrigoo)

comments.author: Forsaken_Leader_8
comments.body: chatgpt is decent for basic syntax, but claude 3.5 sonnet actually tends to be a bit better for pine script because it handles version 5 logic more accurately without getting confused. i’ve been using it to build out scripts and then i use signalwhisper to cross-reference the actual signals it produces. it’s a good way to see if your ai-generated logic actually lines up with what other established ai models are seeing on the charts.
